extra.message: User input 'name' is concatenated directly into SQL query using Python string formatting. An attacker can inject malicious SQL through the student name field. Query: INSERT INTO students (name) VALUES ('%(name)s')

Jinja2 autoescape is disabled (autoescape=False). Multiple template outputs render user-controlled data without escaping: review_text in review.jinja2 line 22, course.title/description in course.jinja2 lines 14-15, student names in students.jinja2 line 16, course titles in courses.jinja2 line 17 and student.jinja2 lines 19-20. This allows stored XSS attacks.
Password hashing uses MD5 which is cryptographically weak and susceptible to collision and rainbow table attacks. Should use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 instead.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of Weak Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: CSRF middleware is commented out (line 27: # csrf_middleware,). All POST endpoints are vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.

The evaluate endpoint (POST /students/{student_id}/evaluate/{course_id}) lacks the @authorize() decorator. While the template checks auth_user.is_admin for UI display, the backend does not enforce admin authorization. Any authenticated user can evaluate any student for any course.
